Store documents as memories with vector: Store documents as memories with vector embeddings and perform similarity-based semantic retrieval using GoodMem
The base URL of your GoodMem API server: The base URL of your GoodMem API server (e.g., https://api.goodmem.ai or http://localhost:8080)
Your GoodMem API key for authentication: Your GoodMem API key for authentication (X-API-Key)
Connect to your GoodMem API instance for: Connect to your GoodMem API instance for vector-based memory storage and semantic retrieval
Create a new space or reuse an existing: Create a new space or reuse an existing one. A space is a logical container for organizing related memories, configured with embedders that convert text to vector embeddings
Store a document as a new memory in a sp: Store a document as a new memory in a space. The memory is processed asynchronously - chunked into searchable pieces and embedded into vectors. Accepts a file or plain text.
Perform similarity-based semantic retrie: Perform similarity-based semantic retrieval across one or more spaces. Returns matching chunks ranked by relevance, with optional full memory definitions.
Fetch a specific memory record by its ID: Fetch a specific memory record by its ID, including metadata, processing status, and optionally the original content.
Permanently delete a memory and its asso: Permanently delete a memory and its associated chunks and vector embeddings.
Author: Author
A unique name for the space. If a space: A unique name for the space. If a space with this name already exists, its ID will be returned instead of creating a duplicate
The embedder model that converts text in: The embedder model that converts text into vector representations for similarity search
A file to store as memory (PDF, DOCX, im: A file to store as memory (PDF, DOCX, image, etc.). Connect the output of a trigger or action that provides a file. Content type is auto-detected from the file extension.
Plain text content to store as memory (s: Plain text content to store as memory (sent as text/plain). If both File and Text Content are provided, the file takes priority.
Where this memory came from (e.g., \"goo: Where this memory came from (e.g., "google-drive", "gmail", "manual upload"). Stored in metadata.source
The author or creator of the content. St: The author or creator of the content. Stored in metadata.author
Comma-separated tags for categorization: Comma-separated tags for categorization (e.g., "legal,research,important"). Stored in metadata.tags as an array
Extra key-value metadata as JSON. Merged: Extra key-value metadata as JSON. Merged with Source, Author, and Tags fields above
A natural language query used to find se: A natural language query used to find semantically similar memory chunks
Select one or more spaces to search acro: Select one or more spaces to search across
Limit the number of returned memories: Limit the number of returned memories
Fetch the full memory metadata (source d: Fetch the full memory metadata (source document info, processing status) alongside the matched chunks
Retry for up to 60 seconds when no resul: Retry for up to 60 seconds when no results are found. Enable this when memories were just added and may still be undergoing chunking and embedding
Optional reranker model to improve resul: Optional reranker model to improve result ordering
Optional LLM to generate contextual resp: Optional LLM to generate contextual responses alongside retrieved chunks
Minimum score (0-1) for including result: Minimum score (0-1) for including results. Only used when Reranker or LLM is set.
Creativity setting for LLM generation (0: Creativity setting for LLM generation (0-2). Only used when LLM ID is set.
Reorder results by creation time instead: Reorder results by creation time instead of relevance score
The UUID of the memory to fetch (returne: The UUID of the memory to fetch (returned by Create Memory)
Fetch the original document content of t: Fetch the original document content of the memory in addition to its metadata
The UUID of the memory to delete (return: The UUID of the memory to delete (returned by Create Memory)
